title: englerina inaequilatera
intro: Englerina inaequilatera, also known as the African mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the family Loranthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Englerina inaequilatera include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Englerina inaequilatera can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or extracting the plant's compounds using solvents, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
